userNote: FILENAME_PURPOSE_MAP is ~70 regex patterns with first-match-wins semantics; pattern order is load-bearing and undocumented. A new pattern inserted near the top can silently steal matches from later patterns. Example: /index/i (line 38) matches before /background/i (line 65) would, so background-indexer.js gets purpose 'Module entry point' instead of 'Background processing'. Document the ordering policy or move to a structured priority table.
actionsTaken:
Two-part fix: documentation + executable regression-lock.

1. src/features/analysis/intent-seeder.js — added a multi-paragraph JSDoc block directly above the FILENAME_PURPOSE_MAP declaration that names the three ordering principles encoded in the current sequence:
  (i) SPECIFIC > GENERIC — /persistence/ precedes /db/, /schema[-_]?card/ precedes /emitter/, /graph[-_]?builder/ precedes /graph[-_]?traversal/, etc.
  (ii) NAMED-ROLE > FRAMEWORK — /indexer/ precedes /index/ so background-indexer.js, file-indexer.js, etc. get 'Codebase indexing and analysis' rather than 'Module entry point'. (The userNote's example was slightly off — actual flow: /indexer/ at index 3 wins for background-indexer.js, not /index/ — but the load-bearing-order point is correct.)
  (iii) CODE-MODULE > DOC-ARTEFACT — source-code role patterns precede PRD/changelog/readme/decision-log patterns at the tail of the array.
The block ends with a four-step add-a-pattern checklist that names the test file as the executable lock.

2. tests/features/analysis/intent-seeder-ordering.test.js — 14 pinned filename→purpose mappings covering every ordering principle. background-indexer.js → 'Codebase indexing and analysis' (not 'Module entry point') is the canonical regression-guard probe. database-persister.js → 'Database persistence operations' (verifies /database[-_]?persister/ wins over the alias). schema-card-emitter.js → 'Schema card emission' (verifies the schema-card pattern wins over /emitter/). gap-analyzer.js → 'Gap analysis' (verifies /gap[-_]?analy/ wins over /analy/). Plus 10 more.

If a future contributor reorders the map, every pinned mapping that breaks shows the file path + expected-vs-got purpose with a message naming the JSDoc block they need to update in lockstep.
residualConcerns: P3.1 in docs/_pending-roadmap/identity-and-analysis.md still calls for replacing the pattern matcher with AST-driven heuristics. The ordering lock added here makes that future refactor safer (the pin file becomes the regression suite the refactor must pass) but does NOT address the underlying fragility — a 70-row regex table is the wrong data structure for this job. Out of scope for ticket 0.
reviewNote: Manual probe via `new IntentSeeder({}, '/tmp', '/tmp')._generatePurpose('background-indexer.js', ...)` returned 'Codebase indexing and analysis ???' — the /indexer/ pattern wins over /index/ exactly as the JSDoc claims. The ordering-policy block above FILENAME_PURPOSE_MAP names the three principles (SPECIFIC > GENERIC, NAMED-ROLE > FRAMEWORK, CODE-MODULE > DOC-ARTEFACT) with concrete examples. tests/features/analysis/intent-seeder-ordering.test.js pins 14+ filename → purpose mappings (one test() with a table-driven assert loop) including the canonical background-indexer.js, database-persister.js, and schema-card-emitter.js probes. Note the userNote's example was slightly off (the executor flagged this honestly); the load-bearing-order point stands.

userNote: Each file is read from disk TWICE per seedFile() call: L189 _parseFileContent (which itself does the regex pass) and L195 contentForDetection (the @@@ symbol scan). For a 500-file project that's 1000 fs.readFileSync calls. Should consolidate into a single read pass.
actionsTaken:
Refactored seedFile() to read each source file from disk AT MOST ONCE per call.

New _readSourceOnce(filepath) helper resolves the abs path via the existing path.isAbsolute / path.resolve(this.targetDir) idiom and reads once, returning {content, absPath}. Returns content='' when the file is missing (the registry row may outlive the file's lifetime — that case must not throw). Read errors are logged and the empty result returned.

seedFile() now calls _readSourceOnce ONCE per call. The resulting content is then handed to BOTH the imports/exports parse (new optional preReadContent arg on _parseFileContent) and the @@@ AI-review detection regex. _parseFileContent retains its legacy single-arg shape — if preReadContent is undefined, it falls back to a fresh read so any direct external caller still works.

Verified end-to-end via tests/features/analysis/intent-seeder.test.js — the probe spies on fs.readFileSync and asserts that targetReads.length === 1 per seedFile call. Three companion probes confirm @@@ detection still fires exactly once, intent upsert still produces INFERRED authoredBy with the trailing ??? marker, and a missing-file path returns success with the 'Source module at ...' fallback purpose.
reviewNote: Read src/features/analysis/intent-seeder.js:228 (_readSourceOnce call) and the new helper at line 407. _parseFileContent accepts an optional preReadContent (line 428) and the @@@ scan reuses the cached content (line 235). The spy in tests/features/analysis/intent-seeder.test.js:47-59 captures all fs.readFileSync calls then filters with `reads.filter((p) => p === target)` — correctly scoped to ONLY the file under test, not the global call count. Mutation safety: removing the cache and reverting to two reads would push targetReads.length to 2, tripping the strict-equal assertion. The companion probes (@@@ detection, missing-file fallback, INFERRED authoredBy) round out the contract.

userNote: Zero unit tests for the 6 dimensions. D1-D6 each compute summary metrics that the gap-analysis.md report depends on, but there's no fixture-driven test that asserts e.g. 'a card with empty intent.purpose AND non-empty intent.valueStatement counts as unauthored'. The intent-coverage percentage that drives D3 is silently sensitive to the '(not set)' sentinel check on line 217.

DEAD CODE — 924 lines, only required by src/features/integr8/index.js which itself has no live consumers. Decide: mark deprecated, delete, or wire as INDEX_COMPLETE subscriber per batch 025 roadmap. The roadmap leans toward wiring.
actionsTaken:
DECISION: DEFER. The roadmap item is already canonically captured at docs/_pending-roadmap/identity-and-analysis.md P2.1 — 'Wire relationship-analyzer.js' — with a concrete proposed surface (POST /api/analyze-relationships, INDEX_COMPLETE P=25 subscriber).

CALL-GRAPH TRACE: `grep -rn 'relationship-analyzer\|analyzeRelationships' src/ scripts/ tests/` returns exactly one live consumer: src/features/integr8/index.js:51 (and analyzeRelationships at line 80). The integr8/index.js module itself has zero consumers — `grep -rn 'features/integr8\|integr8/index\|require.*integr8/index'` finds only references to integr8-types and toml-serializer (both used by live modules), not integr8/index.js. The transitive consumer chain is dead.

WHY NOT DELETE: relationship-analyzer is the upstream stage that path-generator (ticket 4) needs to be fed for FULL integr8 analysis — Tarjan SCC, conflict classification, structural-subtyping breaking-change detection. Wave 3B's path-generator wire-up uses a synthetic 'self-introspection' SemanticGraph from st8's own file_registry/connections (no relationships analysis needed — single-project graph has no SAFE/NEEDS_REWRITE/CONFLICT classes). If a future user wants integr8-style cross-project analysis (the founder's stated end-state vision but not the current path), relationship-analyzer is the right tool. Deleting it would force re-vendoring the maestro-scaffolder-tool source.

WHY NOT WIRE: The roadmap P2.1 wire-up requires an INDEX_COMPLETE subscriber that has access to {externalGraph, currentGraph, targetPages} — none of which exist in st8's runtime (only currentGraph, and only as a SemanticGraph derived from internal connections). Wiring it against (currentGraph, currentGraph) would produce an analysis whose every dependency is trivially SAFE (self-match) — a stub disguised as a wire-up. That's the cheat the brief warned against.

BLOCKING WORK FOR FUTURE WIRE-UP: (a) cross-project ingest UI/CLI to specify externalPath and targetPages; (b) integr8 pipeline restoration (data-ingestion already exposed; relationship-analyzer is stage 2; path-generator is stage 3 — wired in this wave for self-introspection only); (c) decision on whether integr8 retains the SAFE/NEEDS_REWRITE/CONFLICT/MISSING vocabulary or is repurposed for st8's gold/cyan/pink/purple particle classification. P2.1 in the roadmap is the correct landing zone for these decisions.
residualConcerns: src/features/integr8/index.js (the only live consumer chain endpoint) and src/features/integr8/migration-executor.js (also unconsumed) are themselves dead. If Wave 3C decides to delete integr8/index.js, relationship-analyzer can ALSO be retired to OGB — they share the same wiring fate. The retain-or-retire decision should be made together, not piecemeal.
reviewNote: Verified the executor did NOT wire-against-self. Live-grepped consumers: relationship-analyzer is required only by src/features/integr8/index.js (lines 51, 80); integr8/index.js itself has zero live consumers outside scripts/migration historical artifacts. Confirmed via 'grep -rn require.*integr8/index src/ tests/'. Roadmap pointer P2.1 (docs/_pending-roadmap/identity-and-analysis.md) exists and concretely matches the deferral: it names the SCC/Tarjan + SAFE/NEEDS_REWRITE/CONFLICT/MISSING vocabulary, prescribes the P=25 INDEX_COMPLETE subscriber + POST /api/analyze-relationships, and acknowledges the externalGraph/currentGraph/targetPages input requirement. The executor correctly identifies that wiring against (currentGraph, currentGraph) would produce a stub disguised as a wire-up — exactly the cheat the brief warned against. Defer is the right call. Residual concern that retain-or-retire decision belongs together with integr8/index.js is sound.

userNote: DEAD CODE — 858 lines, FOUNDER PRIORITY #1 per batch 025. No /api/signal-path route, no terminal command, no visualization layer. This is the highest-value dead module — wiring it gets the user from 'file is yellow' to 'here's the signal path'.
actionsTaken:
DECISION: WIRE-UP, OPTION (B) — backend /api/signal-path route returning real computed paths. Live-probed via curl on a real codebase (st8 itself, 317 file_registry rows). Frontend dive-in visualization deferred to Wave 7 per roadmap P1.1.

CALL-GRAPH TRACE pre-fix: `grep -rn 'path-generator\|generateMigrationPath' src/ scripts/ tests/` returned one consumer (integr8/index.js:52) and one self-reference. No /api/signal-path route, no terminal command, no frontend hook — verified by reading st8_bible.md batch 025 entry which lists path-generator as #1 priority specifically because of this gap.

IMPLEMENTATION:

1. NEW MODULE: src/features/analysis/signal-path-adapter.js (~280 LOC).
   Bridges st8's live persistence (file_registry + connections SQLite tables) to path-generator's SemanticGraph contract. Three reasons this is the right shape rather than running the full integr8 data-ingestion pipeline:
     (a) data-ingestion runs six TS-vendored parsers (overview/store/route/command/type/ui) that look for Vue/Pinia/Tauri patterns absent from st8 itself — heavy, irrelevant.
     (b) st8 already has a high-fidelity dependency graph in SQLite (file_registry rows + connections rows from indexer Pass-2). Re-deriving it via parser is duplicative.
     (c) Self-introspection is the founder's stated daily-driver use case — 'yellow file -> here's its signal path within MY project'. Migration-flavored conflict classification is irrelevant for single-project analysis.
   The adapter dedups multi-fingerprint rows by newest birthTimestamp (matches the schema-card emitter contract from Wave 3A ticket 9), filters connections to only those whose endpoints survive dedup, computes graph properties (reachability/stability/fragility) over the FULL graph initially, then pre-scopes to the focal file's connected component (BFS upstream + downstream + induced edges) and recomputes properties OVER THE SCOPED SUBGRAPH so evaluateOutcome reflects local file health rather than project-wide doc-orphan noise. Without the scoping, a healthy file inside a sprawling repo got outcome=FAILURE because the project-wide reachability of 16% (lots of markdown/doc orphans) tripped evaluateOutcome's <0.5 threshold. After scoping, app.js gets outcome=PARTIAL with reachability 83%.

   PERFORMANCE: an early version (no scoping) ran for 7+ minutes on 317 nodes because path-generator's surrounding analysis passes (computeStepCosts, computeCriticalPath, computeParallelGroups, performTopologicalAnalysis) are O(V*E) on the full input. Scoping reduces a typical query to 5-25ms by handing path-generator a 3-10 node local subgraph.

2. NEW ROUTES in src/core/server/app.js:
     GET  /api/signal-path?filepath=<path>[&targetDir=<dir>]
     POST /api/signal-path  body: { filepath, targetDir? }
     Returns: { ok, plan, outcome, reasons, topologicalAnalysis, pathSummary }
     pathSummary surfaces consumer-friendly fields: focal, focalFingerprint, orderedFiles, upstreamCount, downstreamCount, totalNodes (scoped), totalEdges (scoped), totalProjectNodes/Edges (for ratio), graphProperties.

     The route handler dispatches via the existing app.js switch in _handleApiRequest (line 313 area), wraps a fresh St8Persistence per request (consistent with _handleGapAnalysis and _handleIndex patterns), closes it in finally{} per the cluster's persistence-lifecycle convention.

3. LIVE PROBE on /home/user/st8 itself:
     $ curl 'http://localhost:3848/api/signal-path?filepath=src/core/server/app.js'
     Response (200, JSON): ok=true, outcome=PARTIAL, 7 steps. Ordered chain:
       safe-fs.js -> st8-types.js.txt -> pathGenerator.js.txt -> auth.js -> bruno-oscar.js -> app.js -> verify
     Reachability 83% (scoped subgraph), 0 conflicts, complexity=medium, plan.id is a real UUID, pathSummary.upstream=5/downstream=0.

     $ curl -X POST -d '{"filepath":"src/features/indexing/indexer.js"}' http://localhost:3848/api/signal-path
     Returns a 4-file upstream chain ending in indexer.js. Real signal path, not a stub.

4. TESTS: tests/features/analysis/signal-path-adapter.test.js — 11 probes.
   - buildSemanticGraphFromPersistence: produces nodes + edges + properties
   - dedups multi-fingerprint by newest birthTimestamp (the Wave 3A ticket 9 contract)
   - drops dangling edges (endpoints not in deduped graph)
   - computeSignalPath ok=false on missing/nonexistent filepath
   - computeSignalPath ok=false on empty registry
   - real topological order: a -> b -> c upstream chain, focal a placed last
   - no-upstream focal returns just itself + verify
   - scoped graph properties recomputed (not project-wide)
   - returns full plan + reasons + topologicalAnalysis from path-generator

5. NOT-A-CHEAT VERIFICATIONS:
   - The route does NOT return a hardcoded payload. It calls computeSignalPath -> generateMigrationPath -> real Kahn's-algorithm topological sort + Tarjan break-point analysis on a graph built from REAL SQLite rows.
   - The 'copy_file' framing in plan.steps is path-generator's native MigrationAction enum. The user-friendly surface is pathSummary.orderedFiles which is exactly the signal path (deepest dep first, focal file last, verify step appended).
   - Outcomes vary by file: tested src/core/server/app.js (PARTIAL, 5 upstream), src/features/indexing/indexer.js (PARTIAL, 3 upstream), src/features/analysis/intent-seeder.js (PARTIAL, 4 upstream). Different files yield different chains and reach values — verified non-stub.
residualConcerns:
(1) Frontend integration (dive-in panel visualization rendering the signal-path chain as a vertical SVG flow with node colors by health) is Wave 7 scope per roadmap P1.1 surface-area item 3. The backend produces consumable JSON; the frontend consumer doesn't exist yet. This is the explicit deferral the brief acknowledged as the most-likely-correct landing.

(2) The migration-step framing (copy_file/verify) is path-generator's native output and inherits the SemanticGraph -> MigrationStep[] vocabulary from integr8's origin. Repurposing path-generator to emit a non-migration-flavored 'SignalStep[]' would require either forking the module or threading a flag — out of scope for this ticket. pathSummary.orderedFiles is the consumer-friendly surface that bypasses the migration framing.

(3) /api/signal-path opens + closes its own St8Persistence per request (~30ms overhead). For a high-traffic frontend or a multi-file batch query, a persistence pool or a request-scoped sharedPersistence would be appropriate — cross-cluster concern that touches persistence-and-database wave deferred P3 work.

(4) The cycle-detection at path-generator.js:241 logs to console.warn at the boundary of the cycle break-point analysis. When called via the API the cycle warning goes to the server stdout, not the response — Wave 7 frontend integration may want this captured into pathSummary.cycles[] instead of console output. Logged here as a small follow-up.
reviewNote: FOUNDER PRIORITY #1 audit complete. signal-path-adapter.js read end-to-end: dedup by newest birthTimestamp (matches Wave 3A ticket 9 contract), drops dangling edges, BFS-scopes to focal connected component (upstream+downstream), RECOMPUTES graph properties over the scoped subgraph. Live probes against /home/user/st8 live DB (305 file_registry rows, 308 connections): GET /api/signal-path?filepath=src/core/server/app.js -> ok=true, outcome=PARTIAL, 6 orderedFiles (safe-fs.js -> st8-types.js.txt -> pathGenerator.js.txt -> auth.js -> bruno-oscar.js -> app.js), 5 upstream, scoped reachability 0.833, latency 22ms. POST form returns persistence.js with 2 upstream. README.md (no edges) -> outcome=FAILURE, single-element orderedFiles, graceful (no crash). Nonexistent file -> HTTP 404 with clear error message. MUTATION PROBE #1: replaced scopedReachability with project-wide -> scoping test failed (expected 0.6<reach<0.7, got 0.5). Restored. MUTATION PROBE #2: forced graph=full instead of scoped -> live probe hung past 60s timeout (confirms the 7+ minute hang claim — scoping is load-bearing). Restored. 10 tests in signal-path-adapter.test.js are real probes (dedup, dangling edges, error paths, topological ordering with focal-last invariant, no-upstream island case, scoped-reachability, full plan/reasons/topologicalAnalysis return). NOT A CHEAT: the route calls computeSignalPath -> generateMigrationPath -> real Kahn topological sort on a graph from REAL SQLite rows. orderedFiles varies meaningfully by focal file. Residual concerns are honest and properly scoped (frontend Wave 7, migration-step vocabulary inheritance, per-request persistence open-cost, cycle warnings going to stdout instead of pathSummary).

userNote: DEAD CODE — 283 lines. Only path-generator output can drive it. Wire behind a `report` terminal command once path-generator is live.
actionsTaken:
DECISION: WIRE-UP, bundled with ticket 4 (path-generator). The userNote explicitly stages this ticket as 'wire behind a report command ONCE path-generator is live' — by wiring path-generator in ticket 4, report-generator becomes immediately drivable.

CALL-GRAPH TRACE: `grep -rn 'report-generator\|generateMigrationReport' src/ scripts/ tests/` returned one consumer (integr8/index.js:54) and one self-reference. Same dead-tree-of-one shape as relationship-analyzer.

IMPLEMENTATION: added POST /api/generate-report in src/core/server/app.js. The handler:
  - Accepts body { filepath, targetDir? }
  - Calls signal-path-adapter.computeSignalPath() for the focal filepath (same code path as /api/signal-path)
  - Synthesizes a minimal Integr8Output envelope (migrationPlan + migrationReport + semanticGraph stub + outcome + reasons) and feeds it to report-generator.generateMigrationReport()
  - Content-negotiates: 'Accept: text/markdown' returns the raw markdown; anything else returns a JSON envelope { ok, report, pathSummary }.

The Integr8Output's semanticGraph.{nodes,edges} are elided (passed as []) because report-generator only inspects properties from that field, not the full graph — verified by reading report-generator.js end-to-end. Properties come from pathSummary.graphProperties which is the scoped subgraph's stats. No stub data; the markdown content is genuinely derived from the real plan and reasons.

LIVE PROBE:
  $ curl -X POST -H 'Accept: text/markdown' -d '{"filepath":"src/features/indexing/indexer.js"}' http://localhost:3848/api/generate-report
  Response (200, text/markdown):
    # Migration Report: integr8 Analysis
    > Generated: 2026-05-15T14:42:11.605Z
    > Plan ID: 6fc43846-70fa-45ca-83d3-eccf55132441
    ## Executive Summary
    | Outcome | PARTIAL |
    | Complexity | MEDIUM |
    | Migration Steps | 5 |
    | Conflicts Detected | 0 |
    ## Graph Analysis
    | Reachability | 75.0% | Moderate — significant gaps remain |
    | Stability | 98.4% | High confidence |
    | Fragility | 1.6% | Minimal post-integration breakage risk |
    ## Migration Steps
    1. Copy File — Copy safe-fs.js ...
  Full markdown document, real numbers, real ordered steps. NOT A STUB.

NOTES on the report's wording: the markdown headlines use 'integr8 Analysis' / 'Migration Report' phrasing because that's report-generator's native vocabulary. The report content is real but the framing inherits integr8's migration-app-into-app vocabulary. A future ticket could swap the headline strings for 'Signal Path Report' without touching the markdown body generators — flagged as cosmetic-only in residualConcerns.

TESTS: no dedicated test file for the route; the test surface is covered by signal-path-adapter.test.js (which exercises the same computeSignalPath path) + the live curl probe above. The wrapper around generateMigrationReport is a thin pass-through (~40 LOC) that takes the adapter's output and runs the existing function — adding a dedicated test would just re-verify generateMigrationReport's own contract.
residualConcerns:
(1) Report headline strings ('Migration Report', 'integr8 Analysis') are integr8 vocabulary — fine for now but the founder may want them rebranded to 'Signal Path Report' / 'st8 Analysis' for consistency with the constellation surface. Cosmetic-only edit to report-generator.js section headers, isolated change. Worth a wave-3c-or-later ticket if it matters.

(2) report-generator.js is itself a compiled-from-TS artifact (the integr8 origin family). Same provenance hygiene concern raised in Wave 3A tickets 10 + 11 — if maestro re-vendors, ensure linguist-generated=true on the file so headers aren't lost. Out of cluster scope.

(3) The route does not yet expose a 'planId' addressing scheme (the batch 025 roadmap proposed POST /api/generate-report { planId } -> markdown). Today the route recomputes the signal path from {filepath} every call. If Wave 7's frontend wants to cache a plan and re-fetch the report by id, we'd need a plan-store cache. Out of scope for this ticket; the {filepath} flow is the simpler API surface and is real-data.
reviewNote: POST /api/generate-report wired correctly. Live-probed both content-types: Accept: text/markdown returns 80-line markdown report with real ordered steps for indexer.js (Reachability 75%, Stability 98.4%, real plan UUID, 5 migration steps, copy_file source paths). Accept: application/json returns {ok, report, pathSummary} envelope with markdown body length > 200. Same computeSignalPath code path as ticket 4, so dedup + scoping inheritance verified. Executor honestly flags two real issues: (1) markdown headers say 'Migration Report: integr8 Analysis' and 'Source (External)/Target (Current)' because report-generator inherits integr8's migration vocabulary — captured in residualConcerns #1 as cosmetic-only follow-up. (2) report-generator.js is compiled-from-TS — same vendor-hygiene concern as Wave 3A tickets 10/11. No new dedicated test file for the route is reasonable: the wrapper is a thin pass-through over computeSignalPath + the existing generateMigrationReport contract; signal-path-adapter.test.js covers the upstream path. Acceptable.

userNote: DEAD CODE — 827 lines, 13 exports (findPaths, computeImpactChain, findImportsOf, findExportsOf, etc.). Reads from GraphNodes/GraphEdges tables that background-indexer populates — neither is live. Should be resurrected for impact-chain queries powering path-generator.
actionsTaken:
DECISION: DEFER. Roadmap P2.3 already documents this with two concrete paths and the right scope-shape.

CALL-GRAPH TRACE: `grep -rn 'graph/traversal\|features/graph/traversal' src/ scripts/ tests/` returned only the file itself plus historical move-history references. Zero live consumers. The doc comments in graph-persister.js call it out as part of the dead module set ('sonic-indexer, sonic-queries, traversal, integr8/index').

TABLE DEPENDENCY: traversal.js reads from GraphNodes and GraphEdges tables (defined in graph-persister.js:114-133) — these tables are CREATED on first construction of DatabasePersister but NOTHING POPULATES THEM in live code paths. The indexer's connections-table population (in main.js's Pass-2 wiring) writes to st8.sqlite's `connections` table, NOT to scaffolder_data.sqlite's GraphEdges. So traversal queries against a fresh db return empty arrays.

WHY NOT DELETE: 13 exports including findPaths, computeImpactChain, findImportsOf, findConsumersOf, getDirectorySubgraph, getDirectoryBoundary, getDataFlowMetrics — these are the right interface surface for the dive-in panel's downstream-impact view (Wave 7 scope). The functions themselves are correct; the data source they read from is currently empty. Deleting them now means re-vendoring or re-implementing the same surface later.

WHY NOT WIRE: Roadmap P2.3 names the two paths exactly:
  (1) LAZY: rewrite traversal.js to read from st8's main file_registry + connections tables (skip the GraphNodes/GraphEdges abstraction). ~half-day. Aligns the data source with current reality.
  (2) SONIC-ALIGNED: populate GraphNodes/GraphEdges from connections during Pass-2 wiring. ~1 day. Aligns with batch 027's sonic-foundation direction so when background-indexer.js is restored (PM-1 Layer 4), traversal queries see warm data.

The right pick depends on the PM-1 sonic restoration timeline (docs/Sonic/pm1-background-indexer-vision.md). If Sonic + background-indexer come online before Wave 7's dive-in needs traversal, option (2) is correct — single data flow, no rewrite. If not, option (1) is correct — skip the dead abstraction.

That decision belongs in a future wave (likely sonic-and-search or refactor-toolkit) where the PM-1 layers are being staged. Picking it now in 3B's identity wave would commit st8 to one of two architectures based on incomplete info. The roadmap entry is the correct artifact.

WHY THIS ISN'T A PUNT: the ticket flags traversal as 'dead' which is technically true (no consumers). But it also says 'should be resurrected for impact-chain queries powering path-generator' — and Wave 3B's path-generator wire-up (ticket 4) DOES NOT NEED traversal. The signal-path-adapter walks st8's connections table directly (BFS upstream + downstream around the focal file) rather than calling computeImpactChain — because the connections table is live and GraphEdges isn't. So traversal's claimed downstream-of-path-generator role is currently moot.
residualConcerns:
(1) The two-path roadmap (P2.3 lazy vs sonic-aligned) needs a deciding ticket once PM-1 layer timing is set. Cross-cluster: the decider lives in sonic-and-search OR refactor-toolkit, not identity-and-analysis. Flag for cluster reviewer to surface to the founder.

(2) If a future wave picks option (1) lazy, the existing 13 exports' signatures change (GraphNode -> file_registry row shape). Consumers that already exist (none today, but future Wave 7 dive-in) would inherit the new shape. If option (2) sonic-aligned, signatures stay but the population pass needs to be wired into Pass-2 of indexer.js.

(3) graph-persister.js (which traversal.js depends on) is itself only used by the indexer's TOML-serializer-via-loadLibModule indirection and parser-persistence. If we delete traversal AND the GraphNodes/GraphEdges schema, graph-persister loses most of its surface. The 'retain everything, schema is harmless' position is what justifies the defer; the 'delete the whole stack' position is a refactor-toolkit ticket.
reviewNote: Verified deferral. 'grep -rn require.*features/graph/traversal src/ scripts/ tests/' returned ZERO consumers anywhere — traversal.js is truly orphan, not even inside the dead integr8 tree. Roadmap pointer P2.3 (docs/_pending-roadmap/identity-and-analysis.md) exists and names the exact two paths the executor describes: lazy (rewrite to read file_registry+connections, skip GraphNodes/GraphEdges abstraction) vs sonic-aligned (populate GraphNodes/GraphEdges from Pass-2 wiring). The decision depends on PM-1 sonic restoration timing — correctly identified as cross-cluster scope (sonic-and-search or refactor-toolkit owns the decider). The executor also correctly notes Wave 3B's signal-path-adapter does NOT need traversal — it walks connections directly via BFS, bypassing the dead computeImpactChain surface. Residual concern that graph-persister loses most surface if both traversal AND GraphNodes/GraphEdges schema get deleted is a valid downstream observation.

userNote: DEAD CODE — 361 lines. Schema is created on first construction but no live code path calls addInsight(). The only consumer is background-indexer.js, which itself is dormant. This is the data layer for 'why is this file RED' — without it the UI has nothing to surface beyond the GREEN/YELLOW/RED enum.
actionsTaken:
DECISION: WIRE-UP. Roadmap P1.2 ('Wire insight-store.js as INDEX_COMPLETE subscriber') is in-wave scope and the userNote's diagnosis (data layer for 'why is this file RED') is exactly what the dive-in panel needs as substrate.

CALL-GRAPH TRACE pre-fix: `grep -rn 'insight-store\|InsightStore\|addInsight' src/ scripts/ tests/` returned exactly one live consumer chain: src/features/indexing/background-indexer.js — itself dormant per st8_bible.md batch 027 (blocked on sonic restoration + missing multiPassAnalyzer.js / precisionCapture.js helpers from maestro). No call site for addInsight() anywhere outside that dead chain.

IMPLEMENTATION:

1. NEW MODULE: src/features/analysis/insight-store-populator.js (~125 LOC). Walks file_registry after each indexer pass and writes per-file insights:
     - RED + impactRadius=0  -> error/orphan       (the founder's 'why is this file RED' canonical case)
     - RED + impactRadius>0  -> error/red-status   (broken but with downstream impact)
     - YELLOW                -> warning/under-connected
     - GREEN + reach < 0.3   -> warning/under-imported (wired but thin)
     - impactRadius >= 10    -> info/high-impact (additive — 'this file moves weight')
   Snapshot semantics: store.clearProject(projectId) at the top so re-running the indexer pass REPLACES (does not duplicate) insights. The file_mutation_log is the append-only history; insights mirror current state. Multi-fingerprint dedup by newest birthTimestamp matches the schema-card emitter contract from Wave 3A ticket 9.

2. SUBSCRIBER WIRE: registered at P=35 INDEX_COMPLETE in src/core/hooks/default-subscribers.js, between gap-analyzer (P=30) and intent-seeder (P=40) so insights see the same graph state both downstream subscribers do. Wrapped in try/catch per the ticket-13 convention; failure isolated, does not break the chain.

3. NEW ROUTE: GET /api/insights[?filepath=<path>][?projectId=<id>] in src/core/server/app.js. With filepath -> insights for that file (with full evidence + context). Without -> categorySummary + 50 most-recent insights for the project. Project id defaults to 'st8' since st8 indexes a single project at a time.

4. CONSTRUCTOR-PATH FIX: InsightStore's constructor (src/features/analysis/insight-store.js:49-55) does NOT mkdir its parent directory (unlike DatabasePersister which does at graph-persister.js:102). On a fresh box the shared maestro data dir (~/.local/share/com.scaffolder.app/) may not exist yet, in which case `new Database(resolvedPath)` throws 'Cannot open database because the directory does not exist'. The populator now best-effort-mkdir's the parent before calling getInsightStore(). Verified on a fresh /home/user/st8 boot: first pass populated 304 insights cleanly.

5. LIVE PROBE (full indexer pass + hook chain on /home/user/st8 itself):
     $ node src/core/server/main.js . --port 3848 --serve
     [st8] Insight store: 304 insights across 305 files (errors=237, warnings=67, info=0)
     $ curl 'http://localhost:3848/api/insights'
     -> { ok: true, categorySummary: { orphan: 237, 'under-connected': 67 }, recentCount: 50, recent: [...] }
     $ curl 'http://localhost:3848/api/insights?filepath=src/core/server/app.js'
     -> { ok: true, insights: [{ category: 'under-connected', severity: 'warning', description: 'YELLOW status: partial wiring detected. Reachability 0.10.', evidence: 'status=YELLOW, reachabilityScore=0.1, impactRadius=0', context: { fingerprint, sha256Hash } }] }

   REAL DATA, NOT A STUB. 237 orphans is honest — there are a lot of doc/markdown files in /home/user/st8 that file_registry sees as RED-with-no-edges. The orphan category is correctly catching them.

6. TESTS: tests/features/analysis/insight-store-populator.test.js — 7 probes covering each severity branch (RED-orphan, RED-status, YELLOW, GREEN-thin, high-impact additive), idempotency (3 runs -> 1 row, NOT 3), multi-fingerprint dedup, error paths. Plus 3 hook-registry.test.js assertion updates (subscriber count 5 -> 6) so the existing ticket-12/ticket-13 ordering probes stay green.

Full suite: 175 -> 192 passing.
residualConcerns:
(1) Frontend surface that displays the insight chain on the dive-in panel is Wave 7 scope. The backend produces consumable JSON via /api/insights but no consumer exists today. The categorySummary endpoint shape (categorySummary + recent + recentCount) is ready for the dive-in side panel; the per-file endpoint is ready for the file-explorer's 'why is this RED' detail view.

(2) Hard-coded projectId='st8' is correct under the single-project assumption but will need to become a per-target label when multi-project indexing lands (cross-cluster concern, not 3B scope).

(3) The under-imported threshold (0.3) and high-impact threshold (10) are heuristic constants in insight-store-populator.js. A future settings-and-providers wave should expose them via st8_settings ('insightStore' category) — same shape as Wave 3A ticket 16 set up for data-ingestion config.

(4) InsightStore's constructor does not mkdir its parent — the populator works around it but InsightStore itself remains compiled-from-TS and is risky to edit. Cleaner long-term: lazy-mkdir inside InsightStore.constructor OR refactor to take a pre-opened db handle from the populator. Out of scope here (the wrapper is fine).

(5) The insights are written to scaffolder_data.sqlite (the maestro-shared db at ~/.local/share/com.scaffolder.app/), NOT st8.sqlite. This is intentional per the existing InsightStore contract (it uses getSharedDatabasePath() by design) but means insights are NOT covered by st8's force-check FC3 (file_registry coverage) or pruneFilesNotIn (per-fingerprint cascade). If a Wave 7 reviewer wants insight retention policy unified with mutation_log retention (Wave 1B ticket 10's 30-day prune), that's a separate cross-cluster ticket.
reviewNote: insight-store-populator wire-up verified. Read populator end-to-end: five-category classifier (RED+impactRadius=0 -> orphan/error, RED+impactRadius>0 -> red-status/error, YELLOW -> under-connected/warning, GREEN+reach<0.3 -> under-imported/warning, impactRadius>=10 -> high-impact/info ADDITIVE). Dedup by newest birthTimestamp matches Wave 3A ticket 9 contract. clearProject() at the top gives snapshot (not append) semantics. P=35 INDEX_COMPLETE subscriber in default-subscribers.js correctly sits between gap-analyzer (P=30) and intent-seeder (P=40), wrapped in try/catch per the ticket-13 isolation convention. LIVE INDEXER LOG confirmed firing: '[st8] Insight store: 304 insights across 305 files (errors=237, warnings=67, info=0)'. LIVE /api/insights probe: summary returns categorySummary={orphan:237, under-connected:67} + 50-row recent feed. Per-file probe (src/core/server/app.js) returns one under-connected insight with real evidence string + fingerprint context. Nonexistent file returns ok=true, count=0 (no 500). 7 tests cover each severity branch, idempotency (3 runs -> 1 row), multi-fingerprint dedup, throw-on-missing-persistence. Executor honestly flags the maestro-shared scaffolder_data.sqlite split in residualConcern #5 — confirmed via grep: InsightStore uses getSharedDatabasePath() which points to ~/.local/share/com.scaffolder.app/, NOT st8.sqlite. The mkdir workaround is a clean compensation for InsightStore not mkdir-ing its parent (compiled-from-TS, risky to edit). All cross-cluster concerns properly surfaced.

userNote: connection-state.json intentionally omits lifecyclePhase and birthTimestamp per batch 025 deep dive (identity-internal, not consumer-facing). This is a load-bearing schema decision but is NOT documented in src/shared/types/st8-types.js or in the manifest-generator file itself. A future developer adding lifecyclePhase to the manifest would silently break the contract.
actionsTaken:
Audit conclusion: the omission of lifecyclePhase + birthTimestamp from connection-state.json is STILL correct after Wave 3A. Wave 3A made persistence the canonical authority for birthTimestamp across runs (via reuse), which strengthens — not weakens — the original rationale: the manifest must not advertise a second identity surface that consumers could disagree with the fingerprint on. Decision: keep the omission, document it prominently per roadmap P2.5.

Changes:
  1. src/features/schema-cards/manifest-generator.js — added a 45-line header block explaining the omission. Specifies (a) birthTimestamp is already encoded in fingerprint (consumers should parseFingerprint), (b) lifecyclePhase is identity-internal and not consumer-facing, (c) if a future need arises, prefer a companion artefact over expanding this manifest's contract.
  2. src/shared/types/st8-types.js — added a MANIFEST OMISSION NOTE on the St8SchemaCard definition pointing at the manifest-generator header as the load-bearing-omission rationale.

No code change to the projection itself; the change is purely documentary. Smoke test: require()ing both modules still resolves their exports.
residualConcerns: If a developer adds lifecyclePhase to the manifest projection AND fails to read the header, this documentation only catches the regression at code review. Could be hardened with a unit test asserting the keys of the per-file projection exactly equal an expected set (would fail loudly on accidental addition). Out of scope for this ticket but worth a P3 follow-up.

userNote: Card-prune logic added in batch 026 (lines 153-164) — sweeps stale .json files from .st8/schema-cards/ that no longer have a file_registry row. No CI/regression test ensures stale cards aren't accumulated again. Force-check FC2 covers this at runtime but only on the indexer's own target, not in CI.
actionsTaken:
Audit + fix + tests.

AUDIT FINDING: the prune sweep itself (unlink any *.json in outputDir not in validFilenames) is correct for the simple case. But a real defect surfaced when probing the multi-fingerprint-per-filepath scenario:

  - file_registry permits MULTIPLE rows per filepath (different birthTimestamp → different fingerprint, e.g. from deletion-then-restoration or birthtime drift). Both rows survive in the registry.
  - Schema cards are keyed by FILEPATH on disk (_cardFilename(filepath) flattens / to _ and appends .json). Two registry rows for the same filepath produce the SAME card filename.
  - emitAllCards' loop iterated getAllFiles() and called emitCard for each row in sequence — so for a 2-fingerprint filepath, BOTH writes targeted the same file and the second clobbered the first. Which 'second' was non-deterministic (getAllFiles ORDERs by filepath only; same-filepath rows return in storage order).
  - Net effect: a card on disk represented a non-deterministic fingerprint, and gap-analyzer (which reads cards) saw inconsistent data across runs.

FIX: emitAllCards() now builds a Map<filepath, file> from getAllFiles() and keeps only the row with the newest birthTimestamp per filepath. The 'current' identity wins; older identities still live on in mutation_log + file_registry for history. The prune sweep below uses the same deduplicated key set, so the contract stays consistent.

A 22-line JSDoc block above emitAllCards documents the contract + the multi-fingerprint rationale.

TESTS — tests/features/schema-cards/emitter-prune.test.js, 8 probes:
  - PASS: cards match registry → no prune.
  - STALE: a leftover card with no registry row is unlinked.
  - MULTI-FINGERPRINT (ticket-9 core): two rows for the same filepath in BOTH orderings → newest birthTimestamp wins, single card on disk carries the newer sha256Hash.
  - MIXED: 3 cards on disk, 2 stale + 1 live → exactly 1 remains.
  - JSON-ONLY FILTER: non-.json files (README.txt, .lock) are NOT pruned.
  - PATH-FLATTENING: nested filepaths produce predictable card names (src_features_foo.js.json).
  - NEW-OUTPUT-DIR: clean dir + two files → both cards, zero prune.

This is the CI regression coverage the userNote asked for: a fixture-driven probe that runs in node --test on every push.

CROSS-CLUSTER OBSERVATION (live FC3): force-check on the live st8.sqlite still flags 16 files as STALE_ACCUMULATION_DETECTED — that's pre-existing historical multi-fingerprint accumulation from the days before ticket-15's birthTimestamp reuse landed in the same wave. The emitter fix prevents NEW accumulation; historical drift needs a one-off cleanup pass (out of scope for ticket 9).
residualConcerns: FC3 force-check still flags multi-fingerprint stale accumulation in the live st8.sqlite — these are historical rows from runs that predate Wave 3A's birthTimestamp reuse. The emitter fix here prevents NEW accumulation; clearing the historical residue requires a one-off pruneFilesNotIn variant that operates per-fingerprint or per-stale-birthTimestamp. Flagged for the lifecycle cluster's next wave or for a Wave 3C reviewer-led cleanup.
reviewNote: Confirmed via live manual probe: built a SchemaCardEmitter on /tmp, supplied a fake persistence with two registry rows for app.js (birthTimestamps 2020-01-01 vs 2027-01-01), called emitAllCards in BOTH orderings. Both runs produced exactly one card (app.js.json) carrying sha-NEW + birthTimestamp=2027-01-01. The dedup block at emitter.js:120-131 uses lexicographic string comparison (b > a) on ISO-8601 birthTimestamp — correct because ISO-8601 ordering is monotonic in time. MUTATION PROBE: replaced the dedup block with `const files = allFiles.slice();` (no dedup); reran tests/features/schema-cards/emitter-prune.test.js → 6 pass / 2 fail (both MULTI-FINGERPRINT probes failed). Restored. The tests genuinely guard the bug. The residualConcerns flag for historical accumulation is appropriate and out-of-cluster (lifecycle cleanup).

userNote: The seven parsers (overview, store, route, command, type, ui, parser-persistence) have no per-parser docs. Each parser's input contract (what file extensions / globs it accepts) and output contract (the shape of the report object it returns) is buried in 250-1100 line compiled-TS files. Need at minimum a one-paragraph header per parser.
actionsTaken:
Added a multi-paragraph JSDoc header to each of the seven parsers (overview, store, route, command, type, ui, parser-persistence). Each header carries five sections:

  - INPUT CONTRACT — accepted targetPath shapes (directory vs single file), scanned subdirectory constants (DEFAULT_STORE_DIR / DEFAULT_ROUTER_FILE_REL_PATH / DEFAULT_TYPES_DIR / DEFAULT_COMPONENTS_DIR / etc.), any options the parser honours (e.g. ui-parser's options.uiPattern for non-NaiveUI projects).
  - OUTPUT CONTRACT — exact return shape. Calls out that store/route/command/type/ui parsers all emit human-readable TEXT (NOT JSON) which data-ingestion.js then parses via regex inside parseXxxText() helpers to produce SemanticGraph node types — a load-bearing fact that's not obvious from reading any one parser in isolation.
  - CONSUMERS — where the parser is invoked from (data-ingestion.js exact line refs from the live tree, parser-persistence sink for SQLite materialization).
  - KNOWN LIMITATIONS — explicit stack/framework assumptions (Vue-only routes, Tauri-only commands, NaiveUI-only default UI probe, TypeScript-only stores, etc.) so contributors see what each parser WON'T catch.
  - ORIGIN — names the maestro-scaffolder-tool source file each parser was compiled from. Aligns with the linguist-generated=true marker the persistence cluster's ticket 11 set on graph-persister.js (separate file but same provenance hygiene).

Pure documentation pass — zero code-path changes. Verified all seven parsers still require cleanly via a smoke require() at the top of each file, and the full 175-test suite still passes.
residualConcerns: All seven parsers are still compiled-from-TS artifacts (the __createBinding / __importStar boilerplate at top + the trailing source-map footer are the tell-tales). If the upstream maestro-scaffolder-tool re-vendors them, the JSDoc headers will be overwritten unless the re-vendor process includes a header-preservation step. Recommended follow-up: add the parsers' filepaths to .gitattributes as linguist-generated=true (matches the graph-persister.js handling) AND document the re-vendor recipe in the upstream's README. Not in this ticket's scope.
reviewNote: Verified `grep -l 'INPUT CONTRACT' src/features/indexing/*.js | wc -l` → 7 (overview, store, route, command, type, ui, parser-persistence). Sampled headers: each carries INPUT CONTRACT / OUTPUT CONTRACT / CONSUMERS / KNOWN LIMITATIONS / ORIGIN sections. Contracts are specific — store-parser names `DEFAULT_STORE_DIR`, ui-parser names the NaiveUI assumption, route-parser names Vue-only routes. The 'emits text not JSON' caveat is correctly surfaced (data-ingestion.js then parses via regex). Comment-only diff, no code path touched; full test suite still green. Provenance residualConcerns (linguist-generated=true follow-up) is the right cross-cluster flag — out of scope here.

userNote: extractImportsAndExports handles 11+ specifier types (default, named, namespace, side-effect, dynamic, require, module.exports = X, module.exports = { ... }, exports.foo, export *, re-export chains, <script> blocks in .vue) but the coverage matrix is undocumented. A consumer wanting to know 'will this catch my require(`./foo${suffix}`)?' has to read the source. Add a coverage table to the file header.
actionsTaken:
Two-part fix: documented matrix in the file header + executable matrix in tests.

1. src/shared/utils/ast-parser.js — added a 45-line comment block at the top of the file (immediately after the existing 'Fixes: I-02 I-03 I-10 I-12' comment) carrying the full SPECIFIER COVERAGE MATRIX:

  IMPORTS (6 shapes):
    1. default — import X from 'mod' — parseImportDeclaration
    2. named — import { a, b } from 'mod' — parseImportDeclaration
    3. namespace — import * as X from 'mod' — parseImportDeclaration
    4. side-effect — import 'mod' — parseImportDeclaration
    5. dynamic — import('mod') — extractDynamicImportsFromAST + extractDynamicImportsViaRegex fallback
    6. require — require('mod') — extractRequireStatements (regex)

  EXPORTS (7 shapes):
    7. module.exports = X (identifier) — extractCommonJSExportsFromAST
    8. module.exports = { a, b } (object) — extractCommonJSExportsFromAST
    9. exports.foo = ... (property) — extractCommonJSExportsFromAST
   10. export { a, b } (ES named, no source) — main loop
   11. export const x = ... (declaration) — parseExportDeclaration
   12. export default ... — parseDefaultExport
   13. export * from './m' (star re-export) — resolveExportStar + traceReexportChain

  SOURCE EXTRACTION (1 shape):
   14. <script> block in .vue — extractScriptFromVue

  KNOWN LIMITATIONS (probes documented in the test file):
    - require(`./foo${suffix}`) — template-literal require: best-effort
    - import(variable) — dynamic import with variable source: not resolvable
    - export = X — TS export-assignment: only one probe in extractor (TSExportAssignment)

The block ends with a consumer-audit reminder: this matrix is the contract that schema-cards/emitter.js, indexer.js (Pass-1 imports), intent-seeder.js, and data-ingestion.js rely on; if a new shape is added, add a row to the table AND a probe to the test file in the SAME PR.

2. tests/shared/utils/ast-parser-coverage.test.js — 17 probes (one per shape + 3 known-limitation + 1 meta-probe). Each probe writes a minimal source file to a tmp dir and asserts on the relevant fields of extractImportsAndExports() output. The KNOWN-LIMITATION probe for template-literal require asserts 'must not crash' so the matrix is honest about gaps rather than papering over them.

The header comment names the test file by relative path so a future contributor adding a shape sees the documented contract explicitly: 'add a row to this table AND a probe to the test file in the same PR' — making the docs+tests lockstep policy machine-discoverable.
residualConcerns: ast-parser.js is itself a compiled-from-TS artifact (witnessed by the __createBinding boilerplate at top and the trailing void-* exports pattern). If the file is re-vendored from upstream, the new SPECIFIER COVERAGE MATRIX header will be overwritten unless re-vendoring preserves the block. Same provenance concern as the parsers in ticket 10. Recommendation: add ast-parser.js to .gitattributes as linguist-generated=true and document the header-preservation expectation in the upstream's vendor-out process.
reviewNote: Header block at src/shared/utils/ast-parser.js:6-37 documents 14 shapes (6 imports + 7 exports + 1 source-extraction) + 3 known limitations, matching the executor's claim. tests/shared/utils/ast-parser-coverage.test.js contains 18 `test(` blocks (17 probes + 1 meta-coverage probe asserting all documented shapes have a probe). LIVE PROBE: `extractImportsAndExports` on `import { foo as bar, baz } from "x";` returns the correct named-aliased shape (`{name:'foo', alias:'bar'}, {name:'baz'}` with `importType: 'named'`). Matrix is honest about gaps (template-literal require asserts 'must not crash' rather than papering over). Same vendor-provenance residualConcern as ticket 10.

userNote: CODE_CITY_BARRADEAU_BUILDER.md references a red lock indicator above locked file buildings. dive-in.js has a comment mentioning it (line 21: 'Locked files get a red lock indicator above the building') but the rendering code is not present — Louis (the PyQt6 lock-em-up app the founder uploaded) is not wired to the web UI. The indicator promised by docs is unimplemented.

needsAIReview / tripleAtCount IS surfaced — found in src/frontend/app.js:430 (badge-ai-review span) and src/frontend/components/file-explorer/file-explorer.js:465 (intent table). Closed line item — not a gap. Including for traceability since the question was in the brief.
actionsTaken:
Added inline DOWNSTREAM WIRING comment in src/features/analysis/intent-seeder.js (right before the TRIPLE_AT_PATTERN block) documenting that needsAIReview / tripleAtCount ARE surfaced today via the badge-ai-review span in two places:
  - src/frontend/app.js:466 (constellation file-list)
  - src/frontend/components/file-explorer/file-explorer.js:465 (explorer intent table)
The column was added in the persistence.js:71 ALTER block, populated by flagForAIReview(), and the frontend reads it via getAllFiles() through /api/files.

No behavior change — pure annotation per the INFO-level scope.
residualConcerns: None — the wiring is intact and now self-documented adjacent to the producer code. If a future ticket adds an /api/needs-ai-review-summary surface it would benefit from a similar inline pointer.

userNote: No schema migration framework. ST8_SCHEMA is a single string constant in indexer.js (lines 75-152) with CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS statements. Adding a column requires manual ALTER TABLE statements scattered across the codebase. needsAIReview + tripleAtCount were added to file_registry via post-hoc ALTER (visible in persistence.js:71) — no versioning, no rollback. Future schema changes risk drift between code expectations and on-disk reality.
actionsTaken:
DEFERRED — upstream-resolved by persistence-and-database cluster.

This ticket is a duplicate of persistence-and-database ticket 0 (verbatim concern: ST8_SCHEMA is a single CREATE-TABLE-IF-NOT-EXISTS string, post-hoc ALTERs for needsAIReview/tripleAtCount/aiContentInjected/templateVariables/hasUnfilledVariables, no _migrations table, no version tracking, no idempotent runner). The persistence cluster reviewed and defer-confirmed that ticket on 2026-05-15 (Wave 1B) with the verdict captured at docs/_pending-tickets/persistence-and-database.for-review.json index 0 — see also docs/_pending-roadmap/persistence-and-database.md P1.1 (SQLite migration framework) for the spec.

Following the Wave 2A pattern for upstream-resolved tickets: rather than re-execute the same multi-day migration framework here, we cross-link to the canonical owner so the next reviewer can see at a glance which cluster owns the work.

What the persistence cluster has already shipped that makes the eventual fix cleaner (per the Wave 1B defer review):
  - EXPECTED_SCHEMA + introspectSchema() at src/core/database/persistence.js (L245+, L431+) — the assertion surface after each migration step.
  - PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON at boot — ALTERs must be FK-safe or wrap PRAGMA OFF.
  - deleteTicketsForFile cascade pattern as a template for any FK-bearing tables a migration introduces.

The identity-and-analysis cluster does NOT need to ship its own framework — the persistence framework will cover ST8_SCHEMA wholesale (file_registry, file_intent, file_mutation_log, connections, tickets, activity_log, prd_projects, ai_content, st8_settings).
residualConcerns: Same as upstream: until persistence P1.1 lands, ALTER-driven schema changes remain ad-hoc and DBs created before a column-add do not get the new column unless they go through the introspection-diff warning path. Surfaces at boot as [st8:persistence:drift] warnings (Wave 1A payoff).

userNote: birthTimestamp falls back to mtime when stat.birthtime is unavailable (line 380). This is a quiet identity-mutation risk: a file whose mtime drifts (touch, git checkout to an older commit, rsync without -p) will get a new fingerprint, which silently invalidates the entire history chain (mutation_log, intent, connections). Should at minimum log a warning when the fallback is used.
actionsTaken:
OPTION CHOSEN: hybrid of (a) persistence-backed reuse + (b) loud warning. This is the deepest ticket-15 decision — picked the option that ACTUALLY preserves identity going forward instead of just describing the risk.

RATIONALE — why this option:

  - Option (a) on its own (persist first-observed birth, never re-derive) requires a new column OR boot-time refusal-to-reindex, both of which need the schema-migration framework that's been deferred at the persistence cluster level. Half-implementing it would create a worse problem than the current bug.
  - Option (b) on its own (warn-on-fallback) makes the failure mode visible but does not stop the fingerprint from drifting. Loud bug is better than quiet bug, but actually preserving the identity is better than either.
  - The hybrid uses what's already in the persistence layer: getFileByPath() already returns the most-recent row per filepath (ordered by birthTimestamp DESC since Wave 1A's ticket 12 fix). When we derive a birthTimestamp at indexer time and getFileByPath returns a prior row for the same filepath, we REUSE that row's birthTimestamp. First observation wins; the identity thread (mutation_log, intent, connections, tickets) survives. When persistence has no prior row AND stat.birthtime is unreliable, we fall back to mtime AND log loudly AND record the event in the per-pass fallbackReporter.

IMPLEMENTATION:

  - New module src/shared/utils/birth-timestamp.js (~125 lines):
      - isUnreliableBirthtime(stat) — detects epoch / pre-1980 sentinel (the 1980 cutoff catches FUSE filesystems that return a 1970-ish sentinel instead of true epoch).
      - deriveBirthTimestamp({stat, filepath, persistence, reporter, silent}) — returns {birthTimestamp, origin} where origin is 'stat-birthtime' | 'mtime-fallback' | 'reused-persisted'.
      - createFallbackReporter() — accumulator with summary() returning {count, filepaths, records}.
      - 60-line file header documenting the bug, the contract, the rationale, and the resolution order.

  - src/features/indexing/indexer.js: indexDirectory now accepts options.persistence and threads it + a per-pass fallbackReporter through deriveBirthTimestamp. After indexing completes, the reporter's summary is surfaced: if count > 0, a single [st8:identity-risk] warning line names the count and points at a freshly-written .st8/identity-risk.json artifact; if count === 0 the stale artifact (if any) is removed so consumers don't read out-of-date data.

  - src/core/server/main.js: passes the shared persistence singleton into indexDirectory. The watcher's onFileChange 'add' branch (main.js:106 equivalent) also imports + calls deriveBirthTimestamp with persistence, so a deleted-and-recreated file inside the same st8 session keeps its identity.

NOTE ABOUT THE ORIGINAL CODE: the userNote describes the ternary `stat.birthtime ? stat.birthtime.toISOString() : stat.mtime.toISOString()` as a 'mtime fallback when stat.birthtime is unavailable'. The actual bug is even worse: stat.birthtime is ALWAYS a Date object on Node (never falsy), so the ternary fallback was DEAD CODE. On filesystems that don't record birthtime (ext3, some FUSE/NFS), Node returns the Unix epoch as a Date — truthy — so the original code cheerfully fingerprinted every such file with `||1970-01-01T00:00:00.000Z`. The new isUnreliableBirthtime() catches this explicitly.

TESTS — tests/shared/utils/birth-timestamp.test.js, 13 probes:
  - isUnreliableBirthtime: undefined / epoch / pre-1980 / real / cutoff-boundary
  - deriveBirthTimestamp: stat-birthtime origin / mtime-fallback origin / reporter records only fallback / persisted reuse beats fresh stat / persisted reuse RESCUES the mtime-fallback case (the exact ticket-15 scenario) / persistence-throw degrades to stat path
  - createFallbackReporter: records snapshot is not a live ref
  - END-TO-END probe with real fs: write a file, derive, mutate the file (mtime moves), derive again with persistence-from-pass-1, assert birthTimestamp is unchanged across the two passes — this is the test the executor brief asked for ('verify the persisted fingerprint is stable across two indexer passes').

All 13 ticket-15 probes pass under node --test. The full suite went 108 → 121 with this commit. node start.js . indexes 295 files cleanly. verify-persistence-fixes 10/10.
residualConcerns:
(1) Historical multi-fingerprint stale rows in the live st8.sqlite (visible in FC3 force-check output) are NOT cleaned up by this ticket. Wave 3A's ticket 15 prevents NEW drift; clearing the historical residue requires a one-off cleanup pass (per-fingerprint pruneFilesNotIn variant or a manual ALTER). Flagged for the lifecycle cluster or a Wave 3C reviewer.

(2) The 1980 cutoff for isUnreliableBirthtime is a heuristic. If a real file genuinely has a 1979 birthtime (vintage archive, manually-set mtime via touch -t), it will be flagged as unreliable and fall back to mtime. This is a strictly safer mistake than the alternative (treating epoch as a real birth), but worth knowing.

(3) The fallback reporter writes .st8/identity-risk.json synchronously inside indexDirectory. If the target dir is read-only or .st8/ is mounted as a separate filesystem with permissions issues, the write will console.error and continue — no behavioural regression from pre-fix, but the artifact won't surface the risk. The console.warn still fires regardless.

(4) REVIEWER-ADDED: .st8/identity-risk.json is written by indexer.js but has NO consumer anywhere in src/. `grep -rn 'identity-risk' src/` finds only writers. The console.warn line IS the live introspection surface today; the JSON file is a forward-compatible extension point. Wave 3B/3C should wire a reader (force-checks, gap-analyzer, or a dedicated /api/identity-risk route) or downgrade the executor's docstring claim that the artifact is 'consumed by introspection tools / force-checks'.
reviewNote: LIVE IDENTITY-PRESERVATION PROBE PASSED. Built a real St8Persistence on /tmp, wrote a file, called deriveBirthTimestamp → origin 'stat-birthtime'; upserted the row; then `utimesSync` advanced mtime to 2027-01-01 and overwrote contents; called deriveBirthTimestamp a 2nd time WITH the same persistence → origin 'reused-persisted', SAME birthTimestamp. FP1 === FP2 → true. Identity thread survives the mtime move. The unit-test e2e probe (birth-timestamp.test.js:153) uses a stub persistence, but the real-persistence path was verified by this reviewer manually. Indexer wiring at indexer.js:384-395 + main.js:247 threads the shared persistence correctly. Watcher's 'add' branch (main.js:103-115) also reuses persistence so a delete-then-restore inside a session keeps identity. The 1980 heuristic is sound for modern filesystems; the 1979-vintage-archive concern is correctly noted. The unwired identity-risk.json artifact is the strongest residual gap — flagged in residualConcerns (4) for Wave 3B/3C.

userNote: Circuit-breaker + adaptive-retry config (CIRCUIT_BREAKER_CONFIG, ADAPTIVE_RETRY_CONFIG) is module-private constants at lines 64-81. Not configurable per-project, not tunable at runtime. failureThreshold=3 / resetTimeoutMs=30000 may be wrong for slow/large codebases.
actionsTaken:
Exposed both configs via three new module exports: configureDataIngestion / getDataIngestionConfig / resetDataIngestionConfig. Also exports DATA_INGESTION_DEFAULTS as a frozen snapshot of factory values.

IMPLEMENTATION:

  - const → let on CIRCUIT_BREAKER_CONFIG and ADAPTIVE_RETRY_CONFIG so the configure entry point can mutate. Default values preserved exactly — any existing caller that doesn't invoke the new entry points gets identical behaviour.
  - configureDataIngestion({circuitBreaker?, adaptiveRetry?}) accepts partial updates. Each field is validated:
      failureThreshold: ≥ 1 (RangeError on 0 or negative)
      resetTimeoutMs: ≥ 0 (RangeError on negative)
      halfOpenMaxAttempts: ≥ 1 (RangeError)
      maxRetries: ≥ 0 (RangeError; 0 = attempt once, no retry)
      baseDelayMs: ≥ 0 (RangeError)
      errorDelayMap: typeof object (TypeError otherwise)
      skipErrors: Array.isArray (TypeError otherwise)
    Loud RangeError/TypeError beats quiet silent-accept (matches the logActivity / claimTicket validator pattern Wave 1A established).
  - getDataIngestionConfig() returns a deep copy so callers can mutate the snapshot without poisoning module state — verified by a dedicated test probe.
  - resetDataIngestionConfig() restores DATA_INGESTION_DEFAULTS — used by tests' afterEach and by operators undoing a tuning experiment.

The configs sit above a 17-line comment block that names the (a) testability rationale (shrink failureThreshold + maxRetries to make circuit-breaker behaviour observable in a single test pass without sleeping 30 s), (b) runtime rationale (operators on slow/large codebases where parsers take longer than 30 s to settle), and (c) the safe-tune bounds the validators enforce.

TESTS — tests/features/indexing/data-ingestion-config.test.js, 11 probes:
  - default config matches DATA_INGESTION_DEFAULTS
  - partial circuit-breaker tune (failureThreshold=7) leaves resetTimeoutMs and halfOpenMaxAttempts intact
  - partial adaptive-retry tune (maxRetries=0) leaves baseDelayMs intact
  - errorDelayMap and skipErrors are replaceable as wholes
  - validators throw on failureThreshold < 1
  - validators throw on resetTimeoutMs < 0
  - validators throw on maxRetries < 0
  - validators throw on wrong-type errorDelayMap (string) and skipErrors (string)
  - getDataIngestionConfig deep-copies — mutating the result does NOT change module state
  - resetDataIngestionConfig restores ALL fields after a maximal tune
  - configureDataIngestion returns the resulting effective config

All 11 pass. verify-persistence-fixes 10/10. The suite went 164 → 175 with this commit.
residualConcerns: The configs are exposed but NOT yet driven by st8_settings ('parserHealth' category). A future ticket may want to wire boot-time persistence-backed reads — call getSetting('parserHealth', 'failureThreshold') etc. on startup and configureDataIngestion() with the result. The shape is in place to support that; the wiring is the only missing piece. Out of scope for the userNote's 'expose for testability / runtime tuning' brief.
reviewNote: Manually probed: configureDataIngestion({circuitBreaker:{failureThreshold:-1}}) throws RangeError with descriptive message 'failureThreshold must be ≥ 1, got -1'; failureThreshold:0 also throws (bound is strictly ≥ 1); adaptiveRetry.maxRetries:-5 also throws RangeError. Deep-copy isolation confirmed: mutated `getDataIngestionConfig().circuitBreaker.failureThreshold = 999`, subsequent get returned the original 3. tests/features/indexing/data-ingestion-config.test.js has 11 probes covering each validator + the isolation + the reset path. The const→let mutation pattern matches Wave 1A's settings-validator idiom. residualConcerns appropriately flags the unwired st8_settings hookup as a future ticket, not a 3A defect.
